evals.prompt: Create guidance for setting up a new TUnit test project on Microsoft.Testing.Platform with the correct project shape, package references, runner behavior, and first smoke test. Identify the package files to consult.
assertions: Routes package installation and project shape through foundation guidance.
Keeps the guidance TUnit-specific and hands broad .NET project concerns to martix-dotnet-csharp only when needed.

evals.prompt: Review a TUnit test suite that uses matrix data, shared class data, Before and After hooks, dependency injection, and parallel execution controls. Identify likely risks and route to the minimum rule set.
assertions: Routes data sources through data rules and maps.
Routes hooks and DI through lifecycle rules.
Calls out parallel-by-default behavior and uses NotInParallel, DependsOn, or limiters only when justified.

evals.prompt: Plan moving a mixed xUnit/NUnit/MSTest suite to TUnit while preserving test behavior, assertion intent, lifecycle setup, and CI reporting. Classify model tier and split the work for parallel agents.
assertions: Treats the initial framework comparison and migration plan as premium or mixed tier.
Uses the framework comparison rule and migration attribute matrix before proposing edits.
Splits follow-up implementation by test project, feature area, or framework source to keep worktree changes isolated.

evals.prompt: Review a TUnit test file and identify assertion issues: unawaited Assert.That calls, use of xUnit/NUnit assertion APIs, async delegates pre-awaited before passing to Assert.That, and missing async Task on test methods. Route to the minimum rule set.
assertions: Identifies unawaited Assert.That as the primary critical risk.
Routes through assertions-fundamentals before any other assertions rule.
Does not pull lifecycle or execution rules unless a cross-workstream concern is present.

evals.prompt: A developer wants to add TUnit.Mocks to their test project and author a custom [Test]-derived attribute that skips slow tests based on an environment variable. Identify what is needed and route to the minimum rule set.
assertions: Gates TUnit.Mocks usage on the C# 14 and beta constraints before any setup guidance.
Routes custom attribute authoring through extending-extension-points before mocking rules.
Does not pull unrelated lifecycle or data rules.

evals.prompt: A user asks for help that is adjacent to martix-tunit but belongs to a different MartiX skill or general Copilot guidance. Assess whether this skill should handle it.
assertions: States whether the request is inside this skill boundary before applying rules.
Does not load unrelated rule files or claim ownership of another skill domain.
Recommends a companion skill or generic guidance path when this skill is not the right fit.
